events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the compliance result directly to the required JSON file. The key issue is that IFGC/NFPA 54 require a sediment trap at gas appliance connections in the listed circumstances, so a missing trap at rough-complete is a non-compliant rough-in defect.

events.label: shell (exit 127)
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && cat > /app/answer.json <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"medium\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 90,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.92,
  \"event_time\": 0,
  \"rate\": 0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Required sediment trap/drip leg is missing from the gas piping serving the appliance or equipment.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Do not approve or close out the rough-in until a compliant sediment trap is installed at the appliance/equipment connection where required.\",
    \"Install the sediment trap in the proper orientation and location ahead of the appliance controls, then retest/inspect the gas piping before placing or leaving the system in service.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"A missing sediment trap/drip leg leaves appliance controls exposed to condensate and debris and does not meet the gas piping installation requirements at rough-complete. The condition must be corrected before acceptance.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"IFGC sediment trap/drip leg requirements for gas appliance connections\",
    \"NFPA 54 / ANSI Z223.1 sediment trap requirements for gas appliance connections\"
  ]
}
EOF
jq . /app/answer.json"
→ /app
total 8
drwxrwxrwx 1 root root 4096 Jul  5 06:10 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 4096 Jul  6 22:34 ..
/bin/sh: 29: jq: not found
